channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: World Cup 2026: Full match schedule, groups, teams and start times
item.description: Al Jazeera's teams, groups, key dates and kickoff times list for World Cup 2026 in Canada, Mexico and the United States.

item.title: How much heat does an AI data centre produce, and where are they located?
item.description: Data centres don't just use large amounts of water and electricity, they're heating the environment too, study finds.

item.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: Haiti forced to change kit over war imagery
item.description: An illustration of the Battle of Vertieres in 1803, with the Haitian flag, had been embedded on the kits.

item.title: Thailand court sentences two men to death for 2015 Bangkok bombing
item.description: The August 2015 attack at the popular Erawan Shrine in Bangkok's commercial heart killed 20 and wounded over 100 more.

item.title: Iran war day 104: Iran attacks US bases, closes strait after Trump strikes
item.description: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz, launches retaliatory strikes on US bases in Kuwait, Bahrain and Jordan after US attacks.

item.title: Why India’s deadly dengue crisis is now no longer confined to the monsoons
item.description: Experts warn that rising temperatures, erratic rainfall and rapid urbanisation have transformed the seasonal disease.

item.title: Knicks one win away from NBA title after record comeback against Spurs
item.description: New York Knicks rally to defeat the San Antonio Spurs by a point in a thrilling Game 4 of the NBA finals series.

item.title: Bolivian farmers clash with police as ex-president vows resistance
item.description: Bolivian farmers clash with police as ex-president vows resistance.

item.title: Thousands rally in Albania in largest protest yet against Kushner resort
item.description: Protesters chant 'Albania is not for sale' as demonstrations swell against Kushner-backed luxury resort plan.

item.title: Funerals held after deadly Pakistani strikes in Afghanistan
item.description: Hundreds gathered in eastern Afghanistan for the funerals of civilians killed in Pakistani airstrikes.

item.title: South Korea fines Coupang $408m over biggest data leak in country’s history
item.description: Data protection watchdog says e-commerce giant failed to implement safety measures and delayed reporting breach.

item.title: Anti-immigrant protesters clash with police, torch car at Belfast rally
item.description: Anti-immigrant demonstrators rallied for another day in Belfast, Northern Ireland, lighting a car on fire.

item.title: One in 70 people worldwide is forcibly displaced: UNHCR
item.description: At least 117.8 million people worldwide remain forcibly displaced due to conflict, violence, and persecution.

item.title: OpenAI says China-based actors stoking opposition to AI data centres
item.description: AI company says ChatGPT accounts sought to 'exploit and amplify existing public concerns' about energy prices.

item.title: Dahiyeh crowds rally in favour of Iranian support against Israel
item.description: Dahiyeh crowds rally in favour of Iranian support against Israel.

item.title: Police in Belfast use water cannon as anti-immigrant unrest continues
item.description: Clashes come as family of knife attack victim calls for calm and condemns violence targeting immigrants.

item.title: FIFA’s Infantino defends US as World Cup host amid visa row, entry denials
item.description: FIFA chief says his organisation cannot rule on government policies after a Somali referee was denied entry into the US.

item.title: Which World Cup teams, players and officials were denied US visas, entry?
item.description: Top Somali referee Omar Artan latest victim of stringent US travel policies after Iranian squad's visas were delayed.

item.title: Nigerian migrants flee South Africa after spike in xenophobic protests
item.description: Migrants say they are living in fear after a group gave people living illegally in South Africa a deadline to leave.

item.title: Iran announces closure of Strait of Hormuz following US strikes
item.description: Iranian officials say the Strait of Hormuz has been closed in response to US strikes.

item.title: FIFA boss tells critics to ‘relax’ over Somali referee deportation
item.description: FIFA boss tells critics to ‘relax’ over Somali referee deportation

item.title: Maasai women turn drought into income through fodder farming in Tanzania
item.description: As drought kills livestock, Maasai women are turning drought-resistant grass into animal feed and income.

item.title: Mexico braces for celebrations, protests as World Cup opens amid tensions
item.description: President Sheinbaum promises safe World Cup as host cities ramp up security after cartel violence scare and protests.

item.title: Iran war live: Tehran targets Bahrain, Kuwait, Jordan after new US strikes
item.description: Iran remains defiant amid US attacks, orders complete closure of Strait of Hormuz to all vessels.

item.title: Iran attacks Bahrain, Kuwait and Jordan again after new waves of US strikes
item.description: Tehran claims attacks on bases in Bahrain, Kuwait and Jordan, as US strikes Karaj and ports in Strait of Hormuz.
